up.method: sole carbon and nitrogen source; consumption not quantified as a flux

sec.method: detected accumulating in spent supernatant of dasBC importer mutant by LC-MS; not quantified as a rate

growth_records.cite: van der Meij et al. Streptomyces venezuelae uses secreted chitinases and a designated ABC transporter to support the competitive saprophytic catabolism of chitin. PLOS Biology (2025). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.3003292. [PMC12370203]
growth_records.snippet: The minimal chitin medium contained, per liter: 5 g of chitin flakes derived from shrimp, 0.6 g of MgSO4.7H2O, 2 g of (NH4)2SO4 (when specified), 150 mL of NaH2PO4/K2HPO4 buffer (0.1 M, pH 6.8), 1 mL of trace elements solution, and 850 mL o
growth_records.notes: Fully defined minimal chitin medium captured from Methods. Growth was assessed only by endpoint CFU counts (wild type ~10^8 CFU/mL vs ~10^6 CFU/mL for chitinase mutants after 6 days) and relative metabolic activity/sporulation - these are order-of-magnitude sporulation endpoints, not time-resolved exponential biomass, so no reliable specific growth rate (mu) or doubling time is derivable and none is reported; growth_rate left null rather than computed to avoid an unreliable value from spore-count endpoints. Substrate uptake (chitin) and product (chitobiose, LC-MS) are described qualitatively, not as fluxes.

up.method: sole carbon source; consumption not quantified as a flux

growth_records.cite: van der Meij et al. Streptomyces venezuelae uses secreted chitinases and a designated ABC transporter to support the competitive saprophytic catabolism of chitin. PLOS Biology (2025). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.3003292. [PMC12370203]
growth_records.snippet: Glucose medium consisted of 5 g of d-glucose and always included 2 g of (NH4)2SO4 as a nitrogen source, replacing the chitin in the minimal chitin medium. Cultures were generally grown in 5 mL volumes within 25 mL glass tubes and incubated
growth_records.notes: Defined minimal glucose medium (5 g/L D-glucose + 2 g/L (NH4)2SO4, otherwise identical base to the minimal chitin medium: 0.6 g/L MgSO4.7H2O, 150 mL/L 0.1 M phosphate buffer pH 6.8, 1 mL/L trace elements, 850 mL/L tap water). Reported as a reference/comparison condition (S. venezuelae grew but was outperformed by chitin in metabolic activity and sporulation). No quantitative growth rate, doubling time, or uptake/secretion flux is reported or derivable (only endpoint biomass/CFU comparisons), so rate fields left null.
